title: Bulk Claim, Verify, and Reject Operations

story.i_want_to: claim, verify, or reject multiple stories in a single API call
story.so_that: I can efficiently manage batches of stories without making N individual requests, reducing latency and enabling atomic batch workflows

rationale: When an orchestrator verifies stories after an agent completes a batch of work, or when an agent claims multiple ready stories for parallel implementation, issuing individual requests is slow and creates partial-success failure modes. Bulk endpoints wrap multiple operations in a single all-or-nothing transaction, ensuring either all operations succeed or none do, while returning per-story results for diagnostics.

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/claim accepts a JSON body with {story_ids: [uuid, ...]} and an agent role API key, sets agent_status=assigned on all specified stories within a single database transaction

POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/verify accepts a JSON body with {stories: [{story_id, result, summary, findings}]} and an orchestrator role API key, sets verified_status=verified on all specified stories within a single database transaction

POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/reject accepts a JSON body with {stories: [{story_id, reason, findings}]} and an orchestrator role API key, sets verified_status=rejected on all specified stories and auto-resets agent_status to pending within a single database transaction
All three bulk endpoints use all-or-nothing semantics: if any individual story fails its precondition check, the entire transaction is rolled back and no stories are modified
Each bulk endpoint returns a response containing per-story results with story_id and status (success or error with reason), even on rollback, so the caller can identify which story caused the failure
Bulk claim validates each story individually: story must have agent_status=pending, all story_dependencies must have verified_status=verified, and all parent epic_dependencies must be satisfied
Bulk verify validates each story individually: story must have agent_status=reported_done before it can be verified
Bulk reject validates each story individually: story must have agent_status=reported_done before it can be rejected, and each rejection entry must include a non-empty reason
All bulk operations create individual audit_log entries for each story affected, recording the actor, previous state, new state, and timestamp
All bulk operations fire individual webhook events for each story affected (story.status_changed, story.verified, or story.rejected) if the tenant has webhooks configured

test_cases.name: Bulk claim succeeds for multiple ready stories
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/claim with {story_ids: [story1.id, story2.id, story3.id]} using agent API key
expected_results: Response body contains results array with 3 entries, each with status: 'success'
All 3 stories now have agent_status=assigned and assigned_agent_id=agent.id
3 audit_log entries are created with action='status_changed'

test_cases.name: Bulk claim rolls back when one story fails precondition
preconditions: fixture(:story, %{epic_id: epic.id, agent_status: :assigned}) as story2 (already assigned)
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/claim with {story_ids: [story1.id, story2.id, story3.id]} using agent API key
expected_results: Response body contains per-story results identifying story2 as the failure (already assigned)
story1 and story3 remain agent_status=pending (transaction rolled back)

test_cases.name: Bulk verify succeeds for multiple reported_done stories
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/verify with {stories: [{story_id: story1.id, result: 'pass', summary: 'All checks pass'}, {story_id: story2.id, result: 'pass', summary: 'Verified'}]} using orchestrator API key
expected_results: Response body contains results array with 2 entries, each with status: 'success'
2 audit_log entries are created with action='verified'

test_cases.name: Bulk reject succeeds and auto-resets agent_status
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/reject with {stories: [{story_id: story1.id, reason: 'Missing tests', findings: []}, {story_id: story2.id, reason: 'No migration', findings: []}]} using orchestrator API key
expected_results: 2 verification_results records are created with result='fail'
Audit log entries record both the rejection and the auto-reset

test_cases.name: Bulk verify rejects request from agent role
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/verify with {stories: [{story_id: story.id, result: 'pass', summary: 'ok'}]} using agent API key
expected_results: Response body contains error indicating insufficient role

test_cases.name: Bulk claim respects story dependency constraints
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/claim with {story_ids: [story1.id, story2.id]} using agent API key
expected_results: Per-story results show story2 failed because its dependency (story1) is not verified
Neither story is claimed (transaction rolled back)

test_cases.name: Bulk reject with missing reason returns validation error
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/reject with {stories: [{story_id: story.id, reason: '', findings: []}]} using orchestrator API key
expected_results: Response body contains validation error indicating reason is required

test_cases.name: Bulk operations fire webhook events per story
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/bulk/verify with 2 stories using orchestrator API key
expected_results: 2 webhook_events records are created, one per story, with event_type='story.verified'
Each webhook_event has the correct story_id in its payload

technical_notes: Use Ecto.Multi to wrap all story updates in a single database transaction for all-or-nothing semantics
Validate each story individually within the Multi pipeline before any updates; collect all validation errors and return them in the per-story results
Lock stories with SELECT FOR UPDATE within the transaction to prevent concurrent claim races
Reuse existing story status transition logic from the single-story endpoints (claim, verify, reject) to ensure consistent validation
Fire webhook events after the transaction commits, not inside it, to avoid delivering events for rolled-back changes
Limit batch size to a configurable maximum (default 50) to prevent oversized transactions
The controller should delegate to a BulkOperations context module that coordinates the Ecto.Multi and returns {:ok, results} | {:error, failed_results}
